content.problemStatement: Rectify the errors in the program to correctly print the last character of the string.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty string S consisting of printable ASCII characters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the last character of the given string on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are three test cases. The last characters are 'f' from "CodeChef", 'd' from "HelloWorld", and 'a' from "a".
For "OpenAI" the last character is 'I'; for "ChatGPT" it is 'T'.
A single‑character string returns that character itself.

solution.approach: For each test case we read the whole line as a string. The last character is at index length-1 (or using the language's built‑in method like back() in C++ or charAt in Java). No further processing is required. This runs in O(|S|) time per test case, which is optimal because we must at least read the input.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all strings) overall, i.e., O(\sum |S|).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the buffer used to store each input string.
